name: Quesillo En Salsa Verde
recipeIngredient: Extra sprigs of cilantro -- for garnish
6 whole scallions, trimmed and charred on a grill until marked, then sliced into 1/2 inch lengths
1 wedge toasted country bread, for serving
recipeInstructions:
1. In a heavy saucepan, bring the water to a boil.
2. Add the tomatillos, 3/4 of the sliced onion, 6 of the chiles, and 6 of the garlic cloves.
3. Simmer the mixture covered for about 30 minutes, or until the tomatillos are tender.
4. Cool slightly and transfer to a blender or food processor.
5. Add the remaining onion, chiles, garlic, cilantro, and salt. Puree the mixture.
6. Preheat the oven to its lowest heat.
7. Heat a well-seasoned flat griddle pan or cast iron skillet over medium heat.
8. Brush each slice of cheese with a little oil, then grill the slices for about 20 seconds on each side using a heavy metal spatula.
9. Grill the cheese in batches, transferring the cooked cheeses to a platter in the oven while you finish cooking. Do not let the cheese melt.
10. Place 2 slices of cheese on each of 6 warmed appetizer plates.
11. Pour the hot tomatillo sauce over the top and serve immediately.
12. Garnish with sprigs of cilantro and charred scallions.
13. Accompany with wedges of toasted country bread.
Yield: 6 servings
